title: Forge sources manifest (manifests/sources.json)
description: Registry of external resource SOURCES — Git or local repos that the federated catalog can sync from. Owned by manager/source.mjs (forge source add|list|remove|sync|trust). Each source is shallow-cloned (by `sync`, later) into the machine-local cache ~/.claude/forge-sources/<id> and pinned in .forge/sources.lock; synced resources populate the discoverable CATALOG but stay INERT until `forge catalog admit` (catalog-until-admitted, ADR-0017). Sources default to trust=untrusted; sync only clones+reads and NEVER executes fetched code (ADR-0017 §security).

schema.description: On-disk schema tag for this manifest.

_note.description: Free-form authoring note; ignored by the source operator.

sources.description: Registered external sources, one record per source id.

id.description: Stable source id; the machine-local cache dir name (~/.claude/forge-sources/<id>) and the lockfile key. Lowercase kebab-ish.

url.description: Clone URL (git) or filesystem path (local) of the source repo.

ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit to track (the sync pins the resolved commit in .forge/sources.lock). Defaults to "main".

kind.description: Source kind: a remote git repo, or a local filesystem path.

addedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the source was registered.

trust.description: Trust level. Every new source starts "untrusted"; `forge source trust <id>` promotes to "reviewed". Untrusted sources are clone+read only; their executable kinds never auto-enable and require a human-gated (T2) admission (ADR-0017 §security).
